draft.introduction: The call of Jeremiah, in which God declares he knew the prophet before he was formed in the womb. This passage became a foundational text for the doctrine of divine foreknowledge and vocation.

translation.text: Now the word of the Lord came to me, saying, “Before I formed you in the womb I knew you, and before you were born I consecrated you; I appointed you a prophet to the nations.” Then I said, “Ah, Lord God! Behold, I do not know how to speak, for I am only a youth.”
translation.original_text: πρὸ τοῦ με πλάσαι σε ἐν κοιλίᾳ ἐπίσταμαί σε

translator_notes.note: “Before I formed you” (πρὸ τοῦ με πλάσαι σε) uses πλάσσω, the same verb used for God forming Adam from clay in Genesis 2:7 (LXX). The echo implies that each prophetic vocation is a new act of creation.
